skill.name: python-fastapi-patterns
skill.description: FastAPI patterns for building Python APIs with routing, Pydantic validation, dependency injection, middleware, and background task examples.
skill.summary: Build FastAPI services with practical Python API patterns and scaffolded endpoint examples.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical combination of network, environment access, and command patterns, but review found documentation examples and a boilerplate generator rather than malicious behavior. Environment settings, URLs, Redis/database strings, and API key names are placeholders for FastAPI applications; command matches are mostly Markdown code fences, with one Bash scaffold script that prints generated code. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, hidden network call, or destructive command path was found.

low_findings.title: Bash scaffold helper accepts unvalidated names
low_findings.description: The scaffold script reads a resource name, derives case variants with tr and sed, and inserts the value into generated Python identifiers and route text. It prints boilerplate to stdout and does not write files, call the network, or execute generated code, but users should provide simple identifier names before copying output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly uses user input in generated output, but the impact is limited because it only prints a template. No direct command injection or file modification path was found.

low_findings.title: Placeholder application settings and permissive examples
low_findings.description: The FastAPI template and references include placeholder database URLs, Redis URLs, an empty API key field, example CORS origins, and a sample secret string. These are legitimate instructional patterns, but copied production code should replace placeholders and tighten CORS, secrets, and deployment settings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain real placeholders and permissive sample settings. They are framed as examples, so this is a low publication warning rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static command and crypto alerts are documentation false positives
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts point to Markdown code fences or inline code examples, not executed shell commands. The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary FastAPI, Pydantic, UUID, or response examples and do not show hashing, encryption, or credential handling misuse.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited locations shows fenced Python examples or model fields, not executable shell backticks or weak cryptographic functions. The semantic context strongly supports dismissal.

content.user_title: Build FastAPI APIs with Proven Patterns
content.value_statement: Building FastAPI services requires consistent choices for validation, dependencies, middleware, and background work. This skill provides reusable Python patterns and a scaffold helper for practical API development.
actual_capabilities: Shows FastAPI application setup with lifespan startup and shutdown patterns.
Provides request and response model examples using Pydantic v2 validation.
Explains dependency injection for database sessions, authentication, settings, and permissions.
Documents middleware patterns for CORS, logging, request IDs, security headers, and rate limiting.
Covers background task patterns with BackgroundTasks, Celery, APScheduler, Redis queues, and asyncio tasks.
Includes a Bash helper that prints CRUD endpoint boilerplate for a named resource.
limitations: Examples use placeholders and must be adapted before production use.
The skill does not provide a complete authentication or authorization system.
Database models, migrations, and ORM setup are outside the included templates.
The scaffold helper prints boilerplate and does not validate generated Python identifiers.

use_cases.title: Create a new API service
use_cases.description: Start a FastAPI project with clear patterns for routing, settings, validation, and lifecycle handling.

use_cases.title: Standardize service conventions
use_cases.description: Use shared examples for middleware, request IDs, CORS, security headers, and operational behavior.

use_cases.title: Study FastAPI architecture
use_cases.description: Learn how dependencies, Pydantic models, routers, and background tasks fit together in API code.

prompt_templates.title: Create a starter API
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new FastAPI project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a small FastAPI service with a health endpoint, Pydantic response model, and lifespan startup and shutdown hooks.

prompt_templates.title: Add validated models
prompt_templates.scenario: Request and response design with Pydantic v2
prompt_templates.prompt: Design request and response models for a user resource with email validation, field limits, and safe response fields.

prompt_templates.title: Wire dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Add FastAPI dependency injection for settings, an async database session, current user lookup, and admin-only access.

prompt_templates.title: Design production middleware
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a middleware plan for CORS, request IDs, structured logging, security headers, error handling, and rate limiting.

output_examples.input: Create a FastAPI user creation endpoint with validation.
output_examples.output: A structured endpoint design with separate create and response models, validated email fields, a 201 success status, and a database creation call placeholder.

output_examples.input: Add middleware for a frontend application and request tracing.
output_examples.output: A middleware configuration plan with explicit frontend origins, request ID propagation, response headers, and logging fields for method, path, status, and duration.

output_examples.input: Explain how to run long video processing jobs from an API.
output_examples.output: A background processing outline that queues the job, returns a task identifier immediately, and exposes a status endpoint for clients.

best_practices: Keep request models, response models, and database models separate when data exposure matters.
Use dependency injection for shared resources such as settings, database sessions, caches, and authenticated users.
Replace all placeholder URLs, secrets, CORS origins, and debug settings before deploying generated examples.
anti_patterns: Do not copy wildcard CORS settings into production services without review.
Do not hardcode real database passwords, API keys, or shared secrets in source files.
Do not expose raw exception details or tracebacks in public API responses.

faq.question: Which FastAPI version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets modern FastAPI patterns with Pydantic v2 and Python 3.10 or newer.

faq.question: Does it create a complete application?
faq.answer: It provides templates and examples, but you must add your project structure, database models, and deployment settings.

faq.question: Can I use it with SQLAlchemy?
faq.answer: Yes. The dependency examples are compatible with async SQLAlchemy when you provide the session factory and models.

faq.question: Does the scaffold script modify files?
faq.answer: No. The script prints endpoint boilerplate to standard output for review and manual integration.

faq.question: Are the sample secrets production ready?
faq.answer: No. Placeholder strings must be replaced with environment-managed secrets and service-specific configuration.

faq.question: Does it include testing patterns?
faq.answer: It includes dependency override examples for tests, but full API test suites are outside this skill.
